scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are known to each other and are on friendly terms. The Defendant, requiring financial assistance for {{PURPOSE_OF_LOAN}}, approached the Plaintiff requesting a hand loan.

6. Acting on the request and reposing trust in the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant on 15.03.2024. The said amount was transferred electronically via NEFT bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456 from the Plaintiff's bank account to the Defendant's bank account. A copy of the bank transaction record is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

7. Though there was no formal written loan agreement executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and his liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages dated {{WHATSAPP_MESSAGE_DATE}}. Copies of the relevant digital communications evidencing the acknowledgment are annexed hereto as Annexure B.

8. It was orally agreed between the parties that the Defendant shall repay the entire loan amount on or before 20.06.2024. The Plaintiff advanced the money based on this oral understanding and the Defendant's assurances.

BREACH OF CONTRACT AND DEFAULT

9. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount on the agreed due date i.e., 20.06.2024, thereby committing a breach of the oral agreement and causing financial hardship to the Plaintiff.

10. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands and requests for repayment of the said amount towards the Defendant from time to time, but the Defendant postponed the matter on one pretext or another and did not discharge his liability.

11. Consequently, the Plaintiff caused to issue a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding amount within the stipulated time. A copy of the said legal notice is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

12. The said legal notice was sent to the Defendant via {{NOTICE_DISPATCH_MODE}} at his aforementioned address residing at Koramangala, Bengaluru, which was received by him. Despite receipt of the same, the Defendant has failed to reply to the notice or repay the amount due.

DAMAGES

13. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realisation of the decree amount.

14. The cause of action for the present suit arose primarily on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as agreed, and subsequently on 01.07.2024 when the legal notice was issued and remained uncomplied, within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

15. The Plaintiff has calculated the interest and damages as per the terms agreed and law applicable, which are due and payable to the Plaintiff, and the Defendant is legally bound to compensate the Plaintiff for the wrongful withholding of the said money.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

17. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024, failed and refused t
